brand.name: Baby Tracker
brand.tagline: Health diary for your little one

history.empty: No entries in the last 30 days

stats.title: Statistics
stats.empty: No data in the last 7 days

growth.title: Growth
growth.empty: No measurements yet. Add weight, height or head circumference to see trends here.

export.failed: Couldn't generate the PDF. Try again from a dev build.

login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Welcome back!
login.noAccount: Don't have an account? Sign up

signup.title: Sign up
signup.subtitle: Create an account to start your diary
signup.emailConfirmInfo: Check your email — we sent you a confirmation link.

delete.title: Delete account?
delete.message: This permanently removes your profile and all tracked data. This cannot be undone.

profileEdit.emailConfirmInfo: Check your inbox — we sent a confirmation link to your new address.

validation.emailInvalidHint: Make sure it looks like you@example.com.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.networkHint: Check your internet connection and try again.

auth.invalidCredentialsHint: Make sure CapsLock is off and try again.
auth.emailNotConfirmedHint: Check your inbox — we sent you a confirmation link.
auth.userExistsHint: Try signing in or resetting your password.
auth.weakPasswordHint: Use at least 6 characters; mixing letters and digits is even better.
auth.rateLimitHint: Wait a few minutes before trying again.
auth.emailInvalidServer: The server didn't accept this address
auth.emailInvalidServerHint: Generic addresses (bob@, test@…) are blocked as spam protection. Use a real email or +alias.

db.rls: You don't have access to this data
db.rlsHint: Your role might not allow this action.
db.uniqueHint: Check whether it was added earlier.
db.fkHint: The child or related object may have been deleted.
db.checkHint: Check the format and allowed values.

home.noChildHint: Add a child to start your diary
home.noEvents: No events yet. Tap an action card above.

logoutConfirm.title: Sign out?
logoutConfirm.message: You'll be returned to the login screen.

empty.title: Nothing here yet
empty.body: Add a child to start tracking feedings, sleep, diapers and growth.

empty.noChild: Pick a child first to see their milestones.

dueNow.message: These are typical for your child's age. Tap one when they reach it.

s2_smile.title: Smiles at people
s2_smile.description: Reacts to faces and voices with a social smile.

s2_calms.title: Calms when held
s2_calms.description: Calms down when picked up, talked to or held.

m2_head_tummy.title: Lifts head briefly on tummy
m2_head_tummy.description: When on tummy, lifts head and turns it side to side.

c2_watches.title: Watches you move
c2_watches.description: Follows you with eyes when you move nearby.

s4_laughs.title: Laughs out loud
s4_laughs.description: Laughs in response to play or funny faces.

s4_seeks_attention.title: Seeks attention
s4_seeks_attention.description: Smiles or moves to get attention from familiar adults.

l4_coos.title: Coos and babbles
l4_coos.description: Makes vowel sounds ("oooh", "aaah") and babbles back when talked to.

l4_turns_voice.title: Turns toward voices
l4_turns_voice.description: Turns head to find the source of a familiar voice.

m4_head_steady.title: Holds head steady
m4_head_steady.description: Keeps head upright when supported in a sitting position.

m4_holds_toy.title: Holds a toy
m4_holds_toy.description: Holds a toy when it is placed in their hand.

m4_push_up.title: Pushes up on elbows
m4_push_up.description: When on tummy, pushes up on forearms.

s6_knows_people.title: Recognizes familiar people
s6_knows_people.description: Knows familiar faces and reacts differently than to strangers.

l6_raspberries.title: Blows raspberries
l6_raspberries.description: Blows raspberries and makes squealing or back-and-forth sounds.

m6_lean_sit.title: Sits leaning on hands
m6_lean_sit.description: Holds a sitting position with hands as support.

c6_reaches.title: Reaches for objects
c6_reaches.description: Reaches for and grabs nearby toys.

c6_mouths.title: Mouths objects to explore
c6_mouths.description: Puts things in mouth to explore textures and shapes.

s9_clingy.title: Shy or clingy with strangers
s9_clingy.description: Sticks close to familiar adults; warms up slowly to new people.

s9_peekaboo.title: Plays peek-a-boo
s9_peekaboo.description: Engages in simple back-and-forth games like peek-a-boo or pat-a-cake.

l9_responds_name.title: Responds to own name
l9_responds_name.description: Looks toward you when name is called.

m9_sits_alone.title: Sits without support
m9_sits_alone.description: Holds a sitting position without using hands for balance.

m9_crawls.title: Crawls or scoots
m9_crawls.description: Moves across the floor on hands and knees, or by scooting.

c9_finds_partly.title: Looks for objects out of sight
c9_finds_partly.description: Searches for things they saw being hidden.

c9_bangs_objects.title: Bangs two objects together
c9_bangs_objects.description: Holds one toy in each hand and bangs them together.

s12_pat_a_cake.title: Plays games like pat-a-cake
s12_pat_a_cake.description: Joins in for back-and-forth games and turn-taking.

l12_waves.title: Waves bye-bye
l12_waves.description: Waves or claps in greetings and farewells.

l12_first_word.title: First word with meaning
l12_first_word.description: Calls a parent "mama" / "dada" or another word for a specific person/thing.

l12_understands_no.title: Understands "no"
l12_understands_no.description: Pauses or stops a behaviour when told "no".

m12_pulls_to_stand.title: Pulls to stand
m12_pulls_to_stand.description: Pulls up to a standing position holding furniture.

m12_cruises.title: Cruises holding furniture
m12_cruises.description: Walks sideways while holding onto furniture.

m12_pincer.title: Picks up with pincer grasp
m12_pincer.description: Picks up small things between thumb and forefinger.

c12_finds_hidden.title: Finds hidden objects
c12_finds_hidden.description: Looks under a cup or blanket for a toy hidden there.

s15_copies_kids.title: Copies other children
s15_copies_kids.description: Watches other children playing and copies what they do.

s15_affection.title: Shows affection
s15_affection.description: Hugs stuffed animals, dolls, or familiar people.

l15_few_words.title: Says a few words
l15_few_words.description: Tries to say one or two words besides "mama" / "dada".

m15_first_steps.title: Takes a few steps alone
m15_first_steps.description: Walks several steps without holding onto anything.

m15_finger_food.title: Feeds self finger food
m15_finger_food.description: Picks up food and brings it to mouth with fingers.

c15_uses_objects.title: Uses everyday objects correctly
c15_uses_objects.description: Pretends to talk on a phone, drink from an empty cup, page through a book.

s18_points_interest.title: Points to show interest
s18_points_interest.description: Points to objects to share what they notice.

s18_helps_undress.title: Helps with undressing
s18_helps_undress.description: Holds out arms or pulls off socks when getting undressed.

l18_three_words.title: Says three or more words
l18_three_words.description: Tries to say at least three words besides "mama" / "dada".

l18_follows_steps.title: Follows simple directions
l18_follows_steps.description: Follows a one-step direction without gestures ("Give it to me").

m18_walks.title: Walks without holding
m18_walks.description: Walks steadily without holding onto a person or object.

m18_scribbles.title: Scribbles
m18_scribbles.description: Makes marks on paper with a crayon.

m18_climbs.title: Climbs on and off furniture
m18_climbs.description: Climbs on and off a couch or chair without help.

c18_pretend_play.title: Imitates chores
c18_pretend_play.description: Pretends to do chores like sweeping or cooking.

s24_notices_others.title: Notices others' feelings
s24_notices_others.description: Looks to see how others feel and reacts to upset friends.

l24_two_words.title: Combines two words
l24_two_words.description: Joins two words into a phrase ("more milk", "go car").

l24_points_book.title: Points to things in book
l24_points_book.description: Points to pictures when asked ("Where is the bear?").

l24_body_parts.title: Points to body parts
l24_body_parts.description: Points to at least two body parts when named.

m24_runs.title: Runs steadily
m24_runs.description: Runs without falling on most attempts.

m24_stairs.title: Walks up stairs with help
m24_stairs.description: Walks up a few stairs holding a hand or rail.

m24_spoon.title: Eats with a spoon
m24_spoon.description: Uses a spoon to eat, with some spilling.

c24_plays_two_toys.title: Plays with multiple toys
c24_plays_two_toys.description: Plays with more than one toy at a time (puts blocks in a truck).

dueNow.message: These are recommended for your child's age.

empty.noChild: Pick a child first to see vaccinations.
empty.noTemplates: Vaccination schedule not loaded. Run the latest database migration to seed it.

new.photoMessage: Pick a source for the photo.
new.permissionDeniedMessage: Allow camera or photo library access in Settings to attach a photo.

trends.title: Growth trends
trends.empty: No measurements yet. Tap Add to record the first one.
trends.needTwoPoints: Add another reading to see the trend

percentileBandHint.below_p3: Your child is among the smallest 3% of children of this age and sex in the WHO reference. Often this is normal family variation, but it's worth discussing with a pediatrician at the next visit.
percentileBandHint.p3_p15: Your child sits on the lower end of the typical range — between the 3rd and 15th percentile. This is generally normal; the trend over time matters more than any single reading.
percentileBandHint.p15_p85: Your child is within the typical range (15th–85th percentile) — where the majority of healthy children fall in the WHO reference.
percentileBandHint.p85_p97: Your child sits on the higher end of the typical range — between the 85th and 97th percentile. This is generally normal; the trend over time matters more than any single reading.
percentileBandHint.above_p97: Your child is among the largest 3% of children of this age and sex in the WHO reference. Often this is normal family variation, but it's worth discussing with a pediatrician at the next visit.

new.endBeforeStart: End time must be after start time
